# Amounts

Generate a wordlist to fuzz amounts or any other numerical values. Based on [Common Security Issues in Financially-Oriented Web Applications](https://research.nccgroup.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/07/common_security_issues_in_financially-orientated_web.pdf).

Bypass minimum and maximum restrictions, cause unintended behavior and errors, etc.

Works with integer and float numerical values.

Tests:

* amount separators such as space, dot, and comma;
* prepending zeros and appending decimal zeros using dot and comma separators;
* prepending and appending positive and negative scopes;
* prepending currency symbols such as `$`, `£`, and `€` - including negative scope;
* embracing amount with brackets such as `()`, `[]`, and `{}` - including expanding them;
* underflows and overflows;
* binary, hexadecimal, and exponential notations;
* bolean, empty, integer minimum and maximum, and other special values,
* various lengths - including negative scope.

Complimentary wordlists:

* [/danielmiessler/SecLists/blob/master/Fuzzing/JSON.Fuzzing.txt](https://github.com/danielmiessler/SecLists/blob/master/Fuzzing/JSON.Fuzzing.txt)

Extend this script to your liking.

Tested on Kali Linux v2021.2 (64-bit).

Made for educational purposes. I hope it will help!

## How to Run

Open your preferred console from [/src/](https://github.com/ivan-sincek/amounts/tree/main/src) and run the commands shown below.

Run the script:

```fundamental
python3 amounts.py
```

## Generated Amounts

```fundamental
python3 amounts.py -min 1 -max 10000 -mid 2200 -o amounts.txt
```
